context.description: A philosophical nonfiction page argues that there are no universally valid academic rules for changed states of awareness and asks whether MRI patterns could be detected across such states. It proposes studying brain activity during altered consciousness and questions why the brain can generate constructs it later does not understand. The page also cites reports from Erowid and mentions DMT and LSD experiences involving geometry and spatial perception.
normalization_notes: German source text normalized into English control-language values.
Mapped generic raw category to book_body_content and subcategory to question_section based on visible question-led philosophical discussion.

fields.section_heading: Questions about altered consciousness and brain patterns

rows.paragraph_text: The text argues that altered states of awareness still lack universally valid academic rules. It asks whether MRI could reveal shared patterns across such states and whether the brain can generate constructs it later cannot explain, citing reports of psychedelic geometry and altered spatial perception.

content.free_text: book_page book_body_content question_section Book page from Bewusstseinsreisen - Version 2 in German. Philosophical discussion of altered consciousness, MRI research questions, brain-generated constructs, and reported psychedelic experiences.
